revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''灸まん'''（きゅうまん）とは、[[金刀比羅宮]]（こんぴらさん）で知られる[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]の'''灸まん...'
revision.text:
'''灸まん'''（きゅうまん）とは、[[金刀比羅宮]]（こんぴらさん）で知られる[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]の'''灸まん本舗石段や'''（株式会社こんぴら堂）で作られている[[和菓子]]で、金刀比羅宮参詣の土産の代表格であるとともに、[[名物かまど]]などとともに香川県を代表する[[土産菓子]]である。
その名のとおり、[[お灸]]（もぐさ）の形状をしており、先が丸く盛り上がっている。鶏卵の黄身を使った餡を使用している。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kyuman.co.jp/ 灸まん本舗石段や]
{{food-stub}}
[[Category:琴平町|きゆうまん]] [[Category:香川県の食文化|きゆうまん]] [[Category:土産菓子|きゆうまん]]

'''灸まん'''（きゅうまん）とは、[[金刀比羅宮]]（こんぴらさん）で知られる[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]の'''灸まん本舗石段や'''（株式会社こんぴら堂）で作られている[[和菓子]]で、金刀比羅宮参詣の土産の代表格であるとともに、[[名物かまど]]などとともに香川県を代表する[[土産菓子]]である。
その名のとおり、お[[灸]]（[[もぐさ]]）の形状をしており、先が丸く盛り上がっている。[[鶏卵]]の黄身を使った[[餡]]を使用している。
石段やは、元は[[1765年]]（[[明和]]2年）に「麻田屋久八」として金刀比羅宮参道の石段に軒を連ねた[[旅籠]]であった。[[天保]]年間に、[[江戸]]の小金井小次郎という[[侠客]]がこの旅籠に泊まり、麻田屋の女中衆に当時麻田屋の名物として知れ渡っていた「金毘羅灸」を据えるよう所望した。小金井は非常に美男子だったため灸を据えるのに女中衆で取り合いになったという。結局灸を据えることになったある女中が特別柔らかい灸を据えたため、小金井は「こいつは甘めぇお灸だ」とは言ったものの、非常にこの灸が気に入ったらしく、以後「金毘羅灸」はますます評判を取ったという。
麻田屋の六代目の主人が旅籠より茶店に商売替えをする際、この逸話にとりお灸の形をした饅頭を創始したという。現在でも石段や本店の建物は金刀比羅宮参道において最も古い建築の一つである。
「灸まん」は[[2002年]]に[[熊本市]]で開かれた第24回[[全国菓子大博覧会]]において、最高賞である名誉総裁賞を受賞している。
ちなみに株式会社こんぴら堂では、「灸まんうどん」として[[讃岐うどん]]の製造販売や、「灸まん美術館」という美術館も運営している。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kyuman.co.jp/ 灸まん本舗石段や]
{{food-stub}}
[[Category:琴平町|きゆうまん]] [[Category:香川県の食文化|きゆうまん]] [[Category:土産菓子|きゆうまん]]

石段やは、元は[[1765年]]（[[明和]]2年）に「麻田屋久八」として金刀比羅宮参道の石段に軒を連ねた[[旅籠]]であった。[[天保]]年間に、[[江戸]]の小金井小次郎という[[侠客]]がこの旅籠に泊まり、麻田屋の女中衆に当時麻田屋の名物として知れ渡っていた「金毘羅灸」を据えるよう所望した。小金井は非常に美男子だったため灸を据えるのに女中衆で取り合いになったという。結局灸を据えることになったある女中が特別柔らかい灸を据えたため、小金井は「こいつは甘めぇお灸だ」とは言ったものの、非常にこの灸が気に入ったらしく、以後「金毘羅灸」はますます評判を取ったという。

'''灸まん'''（きゅうまん）とは、[[金刀比羅宮]]（こんぴらさん）で知られる[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]の'''灸まん本舗石段や'''（株式会社こんぴら堂）で作られている[[和菓子]]で、金刀比羅宮参詣の土産の代表格であるとともに、[[名物かまど]]などとともに香川県を代表する[[土産菓子]]である。

「灸まん」は[[2002年]]に[[熊本市]]で開かれた第24回[[全国菓子大博覧会]]において、最高賞である名誉総裁賞を受賞している。また、[[高松市]]の[[栗林公園]]内の茶亭・掬月亭でも茶菓子として出されている。
ちなみに株式会社こんぴら堂では、「灸まんうどん」として[[讃岐うどん]]の製造販売や、「灸まん美術館」という[[美術館]]の運営も行っている。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kyuman.co.jp/ 灸まん本舗石段や]

[[画像:Kyu-Man.jpg|thumbnail|240px|灸まん]] '''灸まん'''（きゅうまん）は、[[金刀比羅宮]]（こんぴらさん）で知られる[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]の'''灸まん本舗石段や'''（株式会社こんぴら堂）で作られている[[和菓子]]。金刀比羅宮参詣の[[土産]]の代表格であり、また[[名物かまど]]などと並び香川県を代表する[[土産菓子]]でもある。
その名のとおり、お[[灸]]（[[もぐさ]]）の形状をしており、[[饅頭]]の頂上部が丸く盛り上がった、やや[[円錐]]形に近い形をしている。[[鶏卵]]の黄身を使った[[餡]]を使用している。
石段やは、元は[[1765年]]（[[明和]]2年）に「麻田屋久八」として金刀比羅宮参道の石段に軒を連ねた[[旅籠]]であった。[[天保]]年間に、[[江戸]]の小金井小次郎という[[侠客]]がこの旅籠に泊まり、麻田屋の女中衆に当時麻田屋の名物として知れ渡っていた「金毘羅灸」を据えるよう所望した。小金井は非常に美男子だったため灸を据えるのに女中衆で取り合いになったという。結局灸を据えることになったある女中が特別柔らかい灸を据えたため、小金井は「こいつは甘めぇお灸だ」とは言ったものの、非常にこの灸が気に入ったらしく、以後「金毘羅灸」はますます評判を取ったという。

「灸まん」は[[2002年]]に[[熊本市]]で開かれた第24回[[全国菓子大博覧会]]において、最高賞である名誉総裁賞を受賞している。また、[[高松市]]の[[栗林公園]]内の茶亭・掬月亭でも茶菓子として出されている。
ちなみに株式会社こんぴら堂では、「灸まんうどん」として[[讃岐うどん]]の製造販売や、「灸まん美術館」という[[美術館]]（琴平町の北隣の[[善通寺市]]に所在）の運営も行っている。

revision.comment: /* 外部リンク */
revision.text:
[[画像:Kyu-Man.jpg|thumbnail|240px|灸まん]] '''灸まん'''（きゅうまん）は、[[金刀比羅宮]]（こんぴらさん）で知られる[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]の'''灸まん本舗石段や'''（株式会社こんぴら堂）で作られている[[和菓子]]。金刀比羅宮参詣の[[土産]]の代表格であり、また[[名物かまど]]などと並び香川県を代表する[[土産菓子]]でもある。
== 概要 == その名の通り、お[[灸]]（[[もぐさ]]）の形状をしており、[[饅頭]]の頂上部が丸く盛り上がった、やや[[円錐]]形に近い形をしている。[[鶏卵]]の黄身を使った[[餡]]を使用している。
石段やは、元は[[1765年]]（[[明和]]2年）に「麻田屋久八」として金刀比羅宮参道の石段に軒を連ねた[[旅籠]]であった。[[天保]]年間に、[[江戸]]の小金井小次郎という[[侠客]]がこの旅籠に泊まり、麻田屋の女中衆に当時麻田屋の名物として知れ渡っていた「金毘羅灸」を据えるよう所望した。小金井は非常に美男子だったため灸を据えるのに女中衆で取り合いになったという。結局灸を据えることになったある女中が特別柔らかい灸を据えたため、小金井は「こいつは甘めぇお灸だ」とは言ったものの、非常にこの灸が気に入ったらしく、以後「金毘羅灸」はますます評判を取ったという。
麻田屋の六代目の主人が屋号を「石段や」とし、旅籠より茶店に商売替えをする際、この逸話にとりお灸の形をした饅頭を創始したという。現在でも石段や本店の建物は金刀比羅宮参道において最も古い建築の一つである。
「灸まん」は[[2002年]]に[[熊本市]]で開かれた第24回[[全国菓子大博覧会]]において、最高賞である名誉総裁賞を受賞している。また、[[高松市]]の[[栗林公園]]内の茶亭・掬月亭でも茶菓子として出されている。
ちなみに株式会社こんぴら堂では、「灸まんうどん」として[[讃岐うどん]]の製造販売や、「灸まん美術館」という[[美術館]]（琴平町の北隣の[[善通寺市]]に所在）の運営も行っている。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.kyuman.co.jp/ 灸まん本舗石段や]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2024年5月}} [[画像:Kyu-Man.jpg|thumbnail|240px|灸まん]] '''灸まん'''（きゅうまん）は、[[金刀比羅宮]]（こんぴらさん）で知られる[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]の'''灸まん本舗石段や'''（株式会社こんぴら堂）で作られている[[和菓子]]。金刀比羅宮参詣の[[土産]]の代表格であり、また[[名物かまど]]などと並び香川県を代表する[[土産菓子]]でもある。
== 概要 == その名の通り、お[[灸]]（[[もぐさ]]）の形状をしており、[[饅頭]]の頂上部が丸く盛り上がった、やや[[円錐]]形に近い形をしている。[[鶏卵]]の黄身を使った[[餡]]を使用している。パッケージデザインは琴平町出身の画家・[[和田邦坊]]による。
石段やは、元は[[1765年]]（[[明和]]2年）に「麻田屋久八」として金刀比羅宮参道の石段に軒を連ねた[[旅籠]]であった。[[天保]]年間に、[[江戸]]の小金井小次郎という[[侠客]]がこの旅籠に泊まり、麻田屋の女中衆に当時麻田屋の名物として知れ渡っていた「金毘羅灸」を据えるよう所望した。小金井は非常に美男子だったため灸を据えるのに女中衆で取り合いになったという。結局灸を据えることになったある女中が特別柔らかい灸を据えたため、小金井は「こいつは甘めぇお灸だ」とは言ったものの、非常にこの灸が気に入ったらしく、以後「金毘羅灸」はますます評判を取ったという。
麻田屋の六代目の主人が屋号を「石段や」とし、旅籠より茶店に商売替えをする際、この逸話にとりお灸の形をした饅頭を創始したという。現在でも石段や本店の建物は金刀比羅宮参道において最も古い建築の一つである。
「灸まん」は[[2002年]]に[[熊本市]]で開かれた第24回[[全国菓子大博覧会]]において、最高賞である名誉総裁賞を受賞している。また、[[高松市]]の[[栗林公園]]内の茶亭・掬月亭でも茶菓子として出されている。
株式会社こんぴら堂では、「灸まんうどん」として[[讃岐うどん]]の製造販売や、「灸まん美術館」という[[美術館]]（琴平町の北隣の[[善通寺市]]に所在。和田邦坊画業館等を併設）の運営も行っている。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.kyuman.co.jp/ 灸まん本舗石段や]
{{food-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きゆうまん}} [[Category:焼き饅頭]] [[Category:香川県の土産菓子]] [[Category:香川県の食文化]] [[Category:琴平町]] [[Category:金刀比羅宮]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages, need dummy edit imported for attriubtion, if they are redirects the redirects need to be reviewed, adding jawiki link which must be reviewed to see if the page is already present here. Later on the categories should be renamed too.]][[ja:灸まん]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]][[ja:灸まん]][[Category:qq]]

{{出典の明記|date=2024年5月}} [[File:Kyu-Man.jpg|thumbnail|240px|Kyūman]] '''{{ill|Kyūman|ja|灸まん}}''' is a type of **wagashi (Japanese confectionery)** produced by **{{ill|Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya|ja|灸まん本舗石段や}}** (Konpira-dō Co., Ltd.) in **{{ill|Kotohira, Kagawa|ja|琴平町}}**, a town famous for **{{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮}}** (commonly known as **Konpira-san**). It is one of the most **popular souvenirs for visitors to Kotohira Shrine**, alongside **{{ill|Meibutsu Kamado|ja|名物かまど}}**, and is considered a **representative souvenir of Kagawa Prefecture**.
== Overview == As its name suggests, **Kyūman is shaped like an "okyū" (moxibustion)**, resembling **a slightly conical manju (steamed bun)** with a rounded top. The filling consists of **egg yolk-based sweet bean paste**. The package design was created by **painter {{ill|Wada Kunbō|ja|和田邦坊}}**, a native of Kotohira Town.
The shop **Ishidanya** originated as **"Asadaya Kyuhachi,"** a **ryokan (inn)** established along the stone steps leading to Kotohira Shrine in **1765** (Meiwa 2). During the **Tenpō era**, a famous **Edo-period yakuza** named **Koganei Kojirō** stayed at this inn. He requested that the **maidservants** apply the **famous Konpira moxibustion** on him. As Kojirō was a **handsome man**, the **maids competed** for the chance to apply the treatment. Eventually, one of the maids applied a **gentler version of the moxibustion**, to which Kojirō remarked, **"This is a sweet moxibustion"**, a phrase that gained popularity and further boosted the reputation of **Konpira's moxibustion**.
The **sixth-generation owner** of Asadaya renamed the business **Ishidanya** and transitioned from an **inn to a teahouse**. Inspired by this **yakuza legend**, he **created a bun in the shape of a moxibustion cone**, which became the **origin of Kyūman**. To this day, **Ishidanya's main store** remains **one of the oldest buildings along the Kotohira Shrine approach**.
In **2002**, Kyūman received the **Grand Prize (Honorary Presidential Award)** at the **24th National Confectionery Expo** held in **Kumamoto City**. It is also **served as a tea confectionery** at **{{ill|Kikugetsutei|ja|掬月亭}}**, a **teahouse located within {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} in Takamatsu City**.
Besides Kyūman, **Konpira-dō Co., Ltd.** also operates: - **"Kyūman Udon"**, specializing in the production and sale of **{{ill|Sanuki Udon|ja|讃岐うどん}}**. - **"Kyūman Art Museum"**, located in the neighboring city of **{{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|ja|善通寺市}}**, which features an exhibition on **Wada Kunbō's works**.
== External Links == * [http://www.kyuman.co.jp/ Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya - Official Website]

{{出典の明記|date=2024年5月}} [[File:Kyu-Man.jpg|thumbnail|240px|Kyūman]] '''{{ill|Kyūman|ja|灸まん}}''' is a type of **wagashi (Japanese confectionery)** produced by **{{ill|Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya|ja|灸まん本舗石段や}}** (Konpira-dō Co., Ltd.) in **{{ill|Kotohira, Kagawa|ja|琴平町}}**, a town famous for **{{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}** (commonly known as **Konpira-san**). It is one of the most **popular souvenirs for visitors to Kotohira Shrine**, alongside **{{ill|Meibutsu Kamado|ja|名物かまど}}**, and is considered a **representative souvenir of Kagawa Prefecture**.
== Overview == As its name suggests, **Kyūman is shaped like an "okyū" (moxibustion)**, resembling **a slightly conical manju (steamed bun)** with a rounded top. The filling consists of **egg yolk-based sweet bean paste**. The package design was created by **painter {{ill|Wada Kunbō|ja|和田邦坊}}**, a native of Kotohira Town.
The shop **Ishidanya** originated as **"Asadaya Kyuhachi,"** a **ryokan (inn)** established along the stone steps leading to Kotohira Shrine in **1765** (Meiwa 2). During the **Tenpō era**, a famous **Edo-period yakuza** named **Koganei Kojirō** stayed at this inn. He requested that the **maidservants** apply the **famous Konpira moxibustion** on him. As Kojirō was a **handsome man**, the **maids competed** for the chance to apply the treatment. Eventually, one of the maids applied a **gentler version of the moxibustion**, to which Kojirō remarked, **"This is a sweet moxibustion"**, a phrase that gained popularity and further boosted the reputation of **Konpira's moxibustion**.
The **sixth-generation owner** of Asadaya renamed the business **Ishidanya** and transitioned from an **inn to a teahouse**. Inspired by this **yakuza legend**, he **created a bun in the shape of a moxibustion cone**, which became the **origin of Kyūman**. To this day, **Ishidanya's main store** remains **one of the oldest buildings along the Kotohira Shrine approach**.
In **2002**, Kyūman received the **Grand Prize (Honorary Presidential Award)** at the **24th National Confectionery Expo** held in **Kumamoto City**. It is also **served as a tea confectionery** at **{{ill|Kikugetsutei|ja|掬月亭}}**, a **teahouse located within {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} in Takamatsu City**.
Besides Kyūman, **Konpira-dō Co., Ltd.** also operates: - **"Kyūman Udon"**, specializing in the production and sale of **{{ill|Sanuki Udon|ja|讃岐うどん}}**. - **"Kyūman Art Museum"**, located in the neighboring city of **{{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|ja|善通寺市}}**, which features an exhibition on **Wada Kunbō's works**.
== External Links == * [http://www.kyuman.co.jp/ Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya - Official Website]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2024th year 5月}} [[File:Kyu-Man.jpg|thumbnail|240px|Kyūman]] '''{{ill|Kyūman|ja|灸まん}}''' is a type of '''wagashi (Japanese confectionery)''' produced by '''{{ill|Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya|ja|灸まん本舗石段や}}''' (Konpira-dō Co., Ltd.) in '''{{ill|Kotohira, Kagawa|ja|琴平町}}''', a town famous for '''{{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}''' (commonly known as '''Konpira-san'''). It is one of the most '''popular souvenirs for visitors to Kotohira Shrine''', alongside '''{{ill|Meibutsu Kamado|ja|名物かまど}}''', and is considered a '''representative souvenir of Kagawa Prefecture'''.
== Overview == As its name suggests, '''Kyūman is shaped like an "okyū" (moxibustion)''', resembling '''a slightly conical manju (steamed bun)''' with a rounded top. The filling consists of '''egg yolk-based sweet bean paste'''. The package design was created by '''painter {{ill|Wada Kunbō|ja|和田邦坊}}''', a native of Kotohira Town.
The shop '''Ishidanya''' originated as '''"Asadaya Kyuhachi,"''' a '''ryokan (inn)''' established along the stone steps leading to Kotohira Shrine in '''1765''' (Meiwa 2). During the '''Tenpō era''', a famous '''Edo-period yakuza''' named '''Koganei Kojirō''' stayed at this inn. He requested that the '''maidservants''' apply the '''famous Konpira moxibustion''' on him. As Kojirō was a '''handsome man''', the '''maids competed''' for the chance to apply the treatment. Eventually, one of the maids applied a '''gentler version of the moxibustion''', to which Kojirō remarked, '''"This is a sweet moxibustion"''', a phrase that gained popularity and further boosted the reputation of '''Konpira's moxibustion'''.
The '''sixth-generation owner''' of Asadaya renamed the business '''Ishidanya''' and transitioned from an '''inn to a teahouse'''. Inspired by this '''yakuza legend''', he '''created a bun in the shape of a moxibustion cone''', which became the '''origin of Kyūman'''. To this day, '''Ishidanya's main store''' remains '''one of the oldest buildings along the Kotohira Shrine approach'''.
In '''2002''', Kyūman received the '''Grand Prize (Honorary Presidential Award)''' at the '''24th National Confectionery Expo''' held in '''Kumamoto City'''. It is also '''served as a tea confectionery''' at '''{{ill|Kikugetsutei|ja|掬月亭}}''', a '''teahouse located within {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園}} in Takamatsu City'''.
Besides Kyūman, '''Konpira-dō Co., Ltd.''' also operates: - '''"Kyūman Udon"''', specializing in the production and sale of '''{{ill|Sanuki Udon|ja|讃岐うどん}}'''. - '''"Kyūman Art Museum"''', located in the neighboring city of '''{{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|ja|善通寺市}}''', which features an exhibition on '''Wada Kunbō's works'''.
== External Links == * [http://www.kyuman.co.jp/ Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya - Official Website]

== Overview == As its name suggests, '''Kyūman is shaped like an "okyū" (moxibustion)''', resembling '''a slightly conical manju (steamed bun)''' with a rounded top. The filling consists of '''egg yolk-based sweet bean paste'''. The package design was created by '''painter {{ill|Wada Kunbō|ja|和田邦坊|12=draft}}''', a native of Kotohira Town.
The shop '''Ishidanya''' originated as '''"Asadaya Kyuhachi,"''' a '''ryokan (inn)''' established along the stone steps leading to Kotohira Shrine in '''1765''' (Meiwa 2). During the '''Tenpō era''', a famous '''Edo-period yakuza''' named '''Koganei Kojirō''' stayed at this inn. He requested that the '''maidservants''' apply the '''famous Konpira moxibustion''' on him. As Kojirō was a '''handsome man''', the '''maids competed''' for the chance to apply the treatment. Eventually, one of the maids applied a '''gentler version of the moxibustion''', to which Kojirō remarked, '''"This is a sweet moxibustion"''', a phrase that gained popularity and further boosted the reputation of '''Konpira's moxibustion'''.
The '''sixth-generation owner''' of Asadaya renamed the business '''Ishidanya''' and transitioned from an '''inn to a teahouse'''. Inspired by this '''yakuza legend''', he '''created a bun in the shape of a moxibustion cone''', which became the '''origin of Kyūman'''. To this day, '''Ishidanya's main store''' remains '''one of the oldest buildings along the Kotohira Shrine approach'''.
In '''2002''', Kyūman received the '''Grand Prize (Honorary Presidential Award)''' at the '''24th National Confectionery Expo''' held in '''Kumamoto City'''. It is also '''served as a tea confectionery''' at '''{{ill|Kikugetsutei|ja|掬月亭|12=draft}}''', a '''teahouse located within {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園|12=draft}} in Takamatsu City'''.
Besides Kyūman, '''Konpira-dō Co., Ltd.''' also operates: - '''"Kyūman Udon"''', specializing in the production and sale of '''{{ill|Sanuki Udon|ja|讃岐うどん|12=draft}}'''. - '''"Kyūman Art Museum"''', located in the neighboring city of '''{{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|ja|善通寺市|12=draft}}''', which features an exhibition on '''Wada Kunbō's works'''.
== External Links == * [http://www.kyuman.co.jp/ Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya - Official Website]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2024年5月}} [[画像:Kyu-Man.jpg|thumbnail|240px|灸まん]] '''灸まん'''（きゅうまん）は、[[金刀比羅宮]]（こんぴらさん）で知られる[[香川県]][[仲多度郡]][[琴平町]]の'''灸まん本舗石段や'''（株式会社こんぴら堂）で作られている[[和菓子]]。金刀比羅宮参詣の[[土産]]の代表格であり、また[[名物かまど]]などと並び香川県を代表する[[土産菓子]]でもある。
== 概要 == その名の通り、お[[灸]]（[[もぐさ]]）の形状をしており、[[饅頭]]の頂上部が丸く盛り上がった、やや[[円錐]]形に近い形をしている。[[鶏卵]]の黄身を使った[[餡]]を使用している。パッケージデザインは琴平町出身の画家・[[和田邦坊]]による。
石段やは、元は[[1765年]]（[[明和]]2年）に「麻田屋久八」として金刀比羅宮参道の石段に軒を連ねた[[旅籠]]であった。[[天保]]年間に、[[江戸]]の小金井小次郎という[[侠客]]がこの旅籠に泊まり、麻田屋の女中衆に当時麻田屋の名物として知れ渡っていた「金毘羅灸」を据えるよう所望した。小金井は非常に美男子だったため灸を据えるのに女中衆で取り合いになったという。結局灸を据えることになったある女中が特別柔らかい灸を据えたため、小金井は「こいつは甘めぇお灸だ」とは言ったものの、非常にこの灸が気に入ったらしく、以後「金毘羅灸」はますます評判を取ったという。
麻田屋の六代目の主人が屋号を「石段や」とし、旅籠より茶店に商売替えをする際、この逸話にとりお灸の形をした饅頭を創始したという。現在でも石段や本店の建物は金刀比羅宮参道において最も古い建築の一つである。
「灸まん」は[[2002年]]に[[熊本市]]で開かれた第24回[[全国菓子大博覧会]]において、最高賞である名誉総裁賞を受賞している。また、[[高松市]]の[[栗林公園]]内の茶亭・掬月亭でも茶菓子として出されている。
株式会社こんぴら堂では、「灸まんうどん」として[[讃岐うどん]]の製造販売や、「灸まん美術館」という[[美術館]]（琴平町の北隣の[[善通寺市]]に所在。和田邦坊画業館等を併設）の運営も行っている。
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.kyuman.co.jp/ 灸まん本舗石段や]
{{food-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きゆうまん}} [[Category:焼き饅頭]] [[Category:香川県の土産菓子]] [[Category:香川県の食文化]] [[Category:琴平町]] [[Category:金刀比羅宮]]

{{出典の明記|date=2024th year 5月}} [[File:Kyu-Man.jpg|thumbnail|240px|Kyūman]] '''{{ill|Kyūman|ja|灸まん|12=draft}}''' is a type of '''wagashi (Japanese confectionery)''' produced by '''{{ill|Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya|ja|灸まん本舗石段や|12=draft}}''' (Konpira-dō Co., Ltd.) in '''{{ill|Kotohira, Kagawa|ja|琴平町|12=draft}}''', a town famous for '''{{ill|Kotohira-gū|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|12=draft}}''' (commonly known as '''Konpira-san'''). It is one of the most '''popular souvenirs for visitors to Kotohira Shrine''', alongside '''{{ill|Meibutsu Kamado|ja|名物かまど|12=draft}}''', and is considered a '''representative souvenir of Kagawa Prefecture'''.
== Overview == As its name suggests, '''Kyūman is shaped like an "okyū" (moxibustion)''', resembling '''a slightly conical manju (steamed bun)''' with a rounded top. The filling consists of '''egg yolk-based sweet bean paste'''. The package design was created by '''painter {{ill|Wada Kunbō|ja|和田邦坊|12=draft}}''', a native of Kotohira Town.
The shop '''Ishidanya''' originated as '''"Asadaya Kyuhachi,"''' a '''ryokan (inn)''' established along the stone steps leading to Kotohira Shrine in '''1765''' (Meiwa 2). During the '''Tenpō era''', a famous '''Edo-period yakuza''' named '''Koganei Kojirō''' stayed at this inn. He requested that the '''maidservants''' apply the '''famous Konpira moxibustion''' on him. As Kojirō was a '''handsome man''', the '''maids competed''' for the chance to apply the treatment. Eventually, one of the maids applied a '''gentler version of the moxibustion''', to which Kojirō remarked, '''"This is a sweet moxibustion"''', a phrase that gained popularity and further boosted the reputation of '''Konpira's moxibustion'''.
The '''sixth-generation owner''' of Asadaya renamed the business '''Ishidanya''' and transitioned from an '''inn to a teahouse'''. Inspired by this '''yakuza legend''', he '''created a bun in the shape of a moxibustion cone''', which became the '''origin of Kyūman'''. To this day, '''Ishidanya's main store''' remains '''one of the oldest buildings along the Kotohira Shrine approach'''.
In '''2002''', Kyūman received the '''Grand Prize (Honorary Presidential Award)''' at the '''24th National Confectionery Expo''' held in '''Kumamoto City'''. It is also '''served as a tea confectionery''' at '''{{ill|Kikugetsutei|ja|掬月亭|12=draft}}''', a '''teahouse located within {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|ja|栗林公園|12=draft}} in Takamatsu City'''.
Besides Kyūman, '''Konpira-dō Co., Ltd.''' also operates: - '''"Kyūman Udon"''', specializing in the production and sale of '''{{ill|Sanuki Udon|ja|讃岐うどん|12=draft}}'''. - '''"Kyūman Art Museum"''', located in the neighboring city of '''{{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|ja|善通寺市|12=draft}}''', which features an exhibition on '''Wada Kunbō's works'''.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:灸まん]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11567030]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2024th year 5月}} [[File:Kyu-Man.jpg|thumbnail|240px|Kyūman]] {{ill|Kyūman|ja|灸まん|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūman}} is a type of wagashi (Japanese confectionery) produced by {{ill|Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya|ja|灸まん本舗石段や|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Konpira-dō Co., Ltd.) in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira, Kagawa]], a town famous for [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] (commonly known as Konpira-san). It is one of the most popular souvenirs for visitors to Kotohira Shrine, alongside {{ill|Meibutsu Kamado|ja|名物かまど|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meibutsu Kamado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415506}}, and is considered a representative souvenir of Kagawa Prefecture.
== Overview == As its name suggests, Kyūman is shaped like an "okyū" (moxibustion), resembling a slightly conical manju (steamed bun) with a rounded top. The filling consists of egg yolk-based sweet bean paste. The package design was created by painter {{ill|Kunibo Wada|ja|和田邦坊|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Kunbō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28357213}}, a native of Kotohira Town.
The shop Ishidanya originated as "Asadaya Kyuhachi," a ryokan (inn) established along the stone steps leading to Kotohira Shrine in 1765 (Meiwa 2). During the Tenpō era, a famous Edo-period yakuza named Koganei Kojirō stayed at this inn. He requested that the maidservants apply the famous Konpira moxibustion on him. As Kojirō was a handsome man, the maids competed for the chance to apply the treatment. Eventually, one of the maids applied a gentler version of the moxibustion, to which Kojirō remarked, "This is a sweet moxibustion", a phrase that gained popularity and further boosted the reputation of Konpira's moxibustion.
The sixth-generation owner of Asadaya renamed the business Ishidanya and transitioned from an inn to a teahouse. Inspired by this yakuza legend, he created a bun in the shape of a moxibustion cone, which became the origin of Kyūman. To this day, Ishidanya's main store remains one of the oldest buildings along the Kotohira Shrine approach.
In 2002, Kyūman received the Grand Prize (Honorary Presidential Award) at the 24th National Confectionery Expo held in Kumamoto City. It is also served as a tea confectionery at {{ill|Kikugetsutei|ja|掬月亭|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikugetsutei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a teahouse located within [[:en:Ritsurin Garden|Ritsurin Garden]] in Takamatsu City.
Besides Kyūman, Konpira-dō Co., Ltd. also operates: - "Kyūman Udon", specializing in the production and sale of [[:en:Sanuki udon|Sanuki Udon]]. - "Kyūman Art Museum", located in the neighboring city of [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji, Kagawa]], which features an exhibition on Wada Kunbō's works.
== External Links == * [http://www.kyuman.co.jp/ Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya - Official Website]

[[ja:灸まん]] [[Category:Yaki Manju]] [[Category:Confectionery Souvenirs of Kagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Cuisine of Kagawa prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11567030}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:灸まん]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11567030]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2024th year 5月}} [[File:Kyu-Man.jpg|thumbnail|240px|Kyūman]] {{ill|Kyūman|ja|灸まん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūman}} is a type of wagashi (Japanese confectionery) produced by {{ill|Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya|ja|灸まん本舗石段や|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Konpira-dō Co., Ltd.) in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira, Kagawa]], a town famous for [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] (commonly known as Konpira-san). It is one of the most popular souvenirs for visitors to Kotohira Shrine, alongside {{ill|Meibutsu Kamado|ja|名物かまど|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meibutsu Kamado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415506}}, and is considered a representative souvenir of Kagawa Prefecture.
== Overview == As its name suggests, Kyūman is shaped like an "okyū" (moxibustion), resembling a slightly conical manju (steamed bun) with a rounded top. The filling consists of egg yolk-based sweet bean paste. The package design was created by painter {{ill|Kunibo Wada|ja|和田邦坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Kunbō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28357213}}, a native of Kotohira Town.
The shop Ishidanya originated as "Asadaya Kyuhachi," a ryokan (inn) established along the stone steps leading to Kotohira Shrine in 1765 (Meiwa 2). During the Tenpō era, a famous Edo-period yakuza named Koganei Kojirō stayed at this inn. He requested that the maidservants apply the famous Konpira moxibustion on him. As Kojirō was a handsome man, the maids competed for the chance to apply the treatment. Eventually, one of the maids applied a gentler version of the moxibustion, to which Kojirō remarked, "This is a sweet moxibustion", a phrase that gained popularity and further boosted the reputation of Konpira's moxibustion.
The sixth-generation owner of Asadaya renamed the business Ishidanya and transitioned from an inn to a teahouse. Inspired by this yakuza legend, he created a bun in the shape of a moxibustion cone, which became the origin of Kyūman. To this day, Ishidanya's main store remains one of the oldest buildings along the Kotohira Shrine approach.
In 2002, Kyūman received the Grand Prize (Honorary Presidential Award) at the 24th National Confectionery Expo held in Kumamoto City. It is also served as a tea confectionery at {{ill|Kikugetsutei|ja|掬月亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikugetsutei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a teahouse located within [[:en:Ritsurin Garden|Ritsurin Garden]] in Takamatsu City.
Besides Kyūman, Konpira-dō Co., Ltd. also operates: - "Kyūman Udon", specializing in the production and sale of [[:en:Sanuki udon|Sanuki Udon]]. - "Kyūman Art Museum", located in the neighboring city of [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji, Kagawa]], which features an exhibition on Wada Kunbō's works.
== External Links == * [http://www.kyuman.co.jp/ Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya - Official Website]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:灸まん]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:灸まん]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:灸まん]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11567030]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2024th year 5月}} [[File:Kyu-Man.jpg|thumbnail|240px|Kyūman]] {{ill|Kyūman|ja|灸まん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūman}} is a type of wagashi (Japanese confectionery) produced by {{ill|Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya|ja|灸まん本舗石段や|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Konpira-dō Co., Ltd.) in [[:en:Kotohira, Kagawa|Kotohira, Kagawa]], a town famous for [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] (commonly known as Konpira-san). It is one of the most popular souvenirs for visitors to Kotohira Shrine, alongside {{ill|Meibutsu Kamado|ja|名物かまど|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meibutsu Kamado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415506}}, and is considered a representative souvenir of Kagawa Prefecture.
== Overview == As its name suggests, Kyūman is shaped like an "okyū" (moxibustion), resembling a slightly conical manju (steamed bun) with a rounded top. The filling consists of egg yolk-based sweet bean paste. The package design was created by painter {{ill|Kunibo Wada|ja|和田邦坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Kunbō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28357213}}, a native of Kotohira Town.
The shop Ishidanya originated as "Asadaya Kyuhachi," a ryokan (inn) established along the stone steps leading to Kotohira Shrine in 1765 (Meiwa 2). During the Tenpō era, a famous Edo-period yakuza named Koganei Kojirō stayed at this inn. He requested that the maidservants apply the famous Konpira moxibustion on him. As Kojirō was a handsome man, the maids competed for the chance to apply the treatment. Eventually, one of the maids applied a gentler version of the moxibustion, to which Kojirō remarked, "This is a sweet moxibustion", a phrase that gained popularity and further boosted the reputation of Konpira's moxibustion.
The sixth-generation owner of Asadaya renamed the business Ishidanya and transitioned from an inn to a teahouse. Inspired by this yakuza legend, he created a bun in the shape of a moxibustion cone, which became the origin of Kyūman. To this day, Ishidanya's main store remains one of the oldest buildings along the Kotohira Shrine approach.
In 2002, Kyūman received the Grand Prize (Honorary Presidential Award) at the 24th National Confectionery Expo held in Kumamoto City. It is also served as a tea confectionery at {{ill|Kikugetsutei|ja|掬月亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikugetsutei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a teahouse located within [[:en:Ritsurin Garden|Ritsurin Garden]] in Takamatsu City.
Besides Kyūman, Konpira-dō Co., Ltd. also operates: - "Kyūman Udon", specializing in the production and sale of [[:en:Sanuki udon|Sanuki Udon]]. - "Kyūman Art Museum", located in the neighboring city of [[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsūji, Kagawa]], which features an exhibition on Wada Kunbō's works.
== External Links == * [http://www.kyuman.co.jp/ Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya - Official Website]

== Overview == As its name suggests, Kyūman is shaped like an "okyū" (moxibustion), resembling a slightly conical manju (steamed bun) with a rounded top. The filling consists of egg yolk-based sweet bean paste. The package design was created by painter {{ill|Kunibo Wada|ja|和田邦坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Kunbō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28357213}}, a native of Kotohira Town.
The shop Ishidanya originated as "Asadaya Kyuhachi," a ryokan (inn) established along the stone steps leading to Kotohira Shrine in 1765 (Meiwa 2). During the Tenpō era, a famous Edo-period yakuza named Koganei Kojirō stayed at this inn. He requested that the maidservants apply the famous Konpira moxibustion on him. As Kojirō was a handsome man, the maids competed for the chance to apply the treatment. Eventually, one of the maids applied a gentler version of the moxibustion, to which Kojirō remarked, "This is a sweet moxibustion", a phrase that gained popularity and further boosted the reputation of Konpira's moxibustion.
The sixth-generation owner of Asadaya renamed the business Ishidanya and transitioned from an inn to a teahouse. Inspired by this yakuza legend, he created a bun in the shape of a moxibustion cone, which became the origin of Kyūman. To this day, Ishidanya's main store remains one of the oldest buildings along the Kotohira Shrine approach.
In 2002, Kyūman received the Grand Prize (Honorary Presidential Award) at the 24th National Confectionery Expo held in Kumamoto City. It is also served as a tea confectionery at {{ill|Kikugetsutei|ja|掬月亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikugetsutei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a teahouse located within {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|en|Ritsurin Garden|WD=Q1071520|lt=Ritsurin Garden}} in Takamatsu City.
Besides Kyūman, Konpira-dō Co., Ltd. also operates: - "Kyūman Udon", specializing in the production and sale of {{ill|Sanuki udon|en|Sanuki udon|WD=Q1185324|lt=Sanuki Udon}}. - "Kyūman Art Museum", located in the neighboring city of {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|WD=Q191300|lt=Zentsūji, Kagawa}}, which features an exhibition on Wada Kunbō's works.
== External Links == * [http://www.kyuman.co.jp/ Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya - Official Website]

{{Referencesの明記|date=2024th year 5月}} [[File:Kyu-Man.jpg|thumbnail|240px|Kyūman]] {{ill|Kyūman|ja|灸まん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūman}} is a type of wagashi (Japanese confectionery) produced by {{ill|Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya|ja|灸まん本舗石段や|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Konpira-dō Co., Ltd.) in {{ill|Kotohira, Kagawa|en|Kotohira, Kagawa|WD=Q1271483|lt=Kotohira, Kagawa|1=Kotohira, Kagawa}}, a town famous for {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū|1=Kotohira-gū}} (commonly known as Konpira-san). It is one of the most popular souvenirs for visitors to Kotohira Shrine, alongside {{ill|Meibutsu Kamado|ja|名物かまど|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meibutsu Kamado|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11415506|1=Q11415506}}, and is considered a representative souvenir of Kagawa Prefecture.
== Overview == As its name suggests, Kyūman is shaped like an "okyū" (moxibustion), resembling a slightly conical manju (steamed bun) with a rounded top. The filling consists of egg yolk-based sweet bean paste. The package design was created by painter {{ill|Kunibo Wada|ja|和田邦坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Kunbō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28357213|1=Kunibo Wada}}, a native of Kotohira Town.
The shop Ishidanya originated as "Asadaya Kyuhachi," a ryokan (inn) established along the stone steps leading to Kotohira Shrine in 1765 (Meiwa 2). During the Tenpō era, a famous Edo-period yakuza named Koganei Kojirō stayed at this inn. He requested that the maidservants apply the famous Konpira moxibustion on him. As Kojirō was a handsome man, the maids competed for the chance to apply the treatment. Eventually, one of the maids applied a gentler version of the moxibustion, to which Kojirō remarked, "This is a sweet moxibustion", a phrase that gained popularity and further boosted the reputation of Konpira's moxibustion.
The sixth-generation owner of Asadaya renamed the business Ishidanya and transitioned from an inn to a teahouse. Inspired by this yakuza legend, he created a bun in the shape of a moxibustion cone, which became the origin of Kyūman. To this day, Ishidanya's main store remains one of the oldest buildings along the Kotohira Shrine approach.
In 2002, Kyūman received the Grand Prize (Honorary Presidential Award) at the 24th National Confectionery Expo held in Kumamoto City. It is also served as a tea confectionery at {{ill|Kikugetsutei|ja|掬月亭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikugetsutei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a teahouse located within {{ill|Ritsurin Garden|en|Ritsurin Garden|WD=Q1071520|lt=Ritsurin Garden|1=Ritsurin Garden}} in Takamatsu City.
Besides Kyūman, Konpira-dō Co., Ltd. also operates: - "Kyūman Udon", specializing in the production and sale of {{ill|Sanuki udon|en|Sanuki udon|WD=Q1185324|lt=Sanuki Udon|1=Sanuki udon}}. - "Kyūman Art Museum", located in the neighboring city of {{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|WD=Q191300|lt=Zentsūji, Kagawa|1=Zentsūji, Kagawa}}, which features an exhibition on Wada Kunbō's works.
== External Links == * [http://www.kyuman.co.jp/ Kyūman Honpo Ishidanya - Official Website]
